subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Market Builders combine supply crates by adding tens and ones accurately.
lesson.mini_lesson: Add two-digit numbers by place value. Line up tens and ones, add ones first, and regroup 10 ones as 1 ten when needed.
lesson.objective: Add two-digit numbers within 100 using place value, drawings, number lines, and strategies.
lesson.concept_explanation: Two-digit addition works by combining ones with ones and tens with tens. If the ones make 10 or more, trade 10 ones for 1 ten.
lesson.common_misconception: A child may add digits in the wrong place or forget to regroup 10 ones as 1 ten.
lesson.real_world_connection: Adding within 100 helps combine scores, classroom supplies, and money-like quantities.
lesson.lesson_summary: Add ones, regroup if needed, add tens, and check the sum.
hints: Line up tens with tens and ones with ones.
Add the ones first. Regroup 10 ones as 1 ten if needed.
Then add the tens and write the total.

worked_examples.text: 34 + 25 = 59 because 3 tens + 2 tens = 5 tens and 4 ones + 5 ones = 9 ones.
47 + 36 = 83 because 7 ones + 6 ones = 13 ones, so regroup 10 ones as 1 ten.
On a number line, 28 + 30 + 4 lands on 62.

hint_ladder: Line up tens with tens and ones with ones.
Add the ones first. Regroup 10 ones as 1 ten if needed.
Then add the tens and write the total.

misconception_bank.regrouping_confusion: Learner may forget to trade 10 ones for 1 ten.
misconception_bank.ones_tens_alignment_error: Learner may add ones to tens because numbers are not aligned.
misconception_bank.count_all_overuse: Learner counts every object instead of using tens/ones.
misconception_bank.place_value_addition_error: Learner may ignore place value when combining tens and ones.

game_theme.title: Add Within 100
game_theme.mission_intro: Help the Market Builders combine supply crates by adding tens and ones accurately.

hints: Focus: In 20 + 30, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add the ones (0 + 0) and the tens (2 + 3) by place value.
Verify: The combined place values are 5 tens and 0 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

guided_practice.explanation: Combine like place values: 2 tens + 3 tens = 5 tens, and 0 ones + 0 ones = 0 ones. Therefore, 20 + 30 = 50. Check: 50 - 30 = 20.

question_audio.text: Add 20 plus 30. What is the sum? Base-ten blocks show 20 and 30 as tens and ones to combine into 50.
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.visual_description: Base-ten blocks show 20 and 30 as tens and ones to combine into 50.
guided_practice.accessible_description: Base-ten blocks show 20 and 30 as tens and ones to combine into 50.

hints: Focus: In 40 + 20, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add the ones (0 + 0) and the tens (4 + 2) by place value.
Verify: The combined place values are 6 tens and 0 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

guided_practice.explanation: Combine like place values: 4 tens + 2 tens = 6 tens, and 0 ones + 0 ones = 0 ones. Therefore, 40 + 20 = 60. Check: 60 - 20 = 40.

question_audio.text: Add 40 plus 20. What is the sum? A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 40 plus 20, with a total of 60.
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.visual_description: A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 40 plus 20, with a total of 60.
guided_practice.accessible_description: A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 40 plus 20, with a total of 60.

hints: Focus: In 10 + 70, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add the ones (0 + 0) and the tens (1 + 7) by place value.
Verify: The combined place values are 8 tens and 0 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

guided_practice.explanation: Combine like place values: 1 tens + 7 tens = 8 tens, and 0 ones + 0 ones = 0 ones. Therefore, 10 + 70 = 80. Check: 80 - 70 = 10.

question_audio.text: Add 10 plus 70. What is the sum? A number line starts at 10 and shows a forward jump of 70 to 80.
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.visual_description: A number line starts at 10 and shows a forward jump of 70 to 80.
guided_practice.accessible_description: A number line starts at 10 and shows a forward jump of 70 to 80.

hints: Focus: In 30 + 50, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add the ones (0 + 0) and the tens (3 + 5) by place value.
Verify: The combined place values are 8 tens and 0 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

guided_practice.explanation: Combine like place values: 3 tens + 5 tens = 8 tens, and 0 ones + 0 ones = 0 ones. Therefore, 30 + 50 = 80. Check: 80 - 50 = 30.

question_audio.text: Add 30 plus 50. What is the sum? Base-ten blocks show 30 and 50 as tens and ones to combine into 80.
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.visual_description: Base-ten blocks show 30 and 50 as tens and ones to combine into 80.
guided_practice.accessible_description: Base-ten blocks show 30 and 50 as tens and ones to combine into 80.

hints: Focus: In 47 + 36, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add 7 and 6 to get 13 ones; trade 10 ones for 1 ten.
Verify: The combined place values are 8 tens and 3 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: 7 ones + 6 ones = 13 ones. Trade 10 ones for 1 ten, leaving 3 ones. Then 7 tens + 1 regrouped ten = 8 tens, so 47 + 36 = 83. Check: 83 - 36 = 47.

question_audio.text: Add 47 plus 36. What is the sum? Base-ten blocks show 47 and 36 as tens and ones to combine into 83.
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.visual_description: Base-ten blocks show 47 and 36 as tens and ones to combine into 83.
adaptive_question_bank.accessible_description: Base-ten blocks show 47 and 36 as tens and ones to combine into 83.

hints: Focus: In 28 + 34, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add 8 and 4 to get 12 ones; trade 10 ones for 1 ten.
Verify: The combined place values are 6 tens and 2 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: 8 ones + 4 ones = 12 ones. Trade 10 ones for 1 ten, leaving 2 ones. Then 5 tens + 1 regrouped ten = 6 tens, so 28 + 34 = 62. Check: 62 - 34 = 28.

question_audio.text: Add 28 plus 34. What is the sum? A number line starts at 28 and shows a forward jump of 34 to 62.
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.visual_description: A number line starts at 28 and shows a forward jump of 34 to 62.
adaptive_question_bank.accessible_description: A number line starts at 28 and shows a forward jump of 34 to 62.

hints: Focus: In 56 + 27, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add 6 and 7 to get 13 ones; trade 10 ones for 1 ten.
Verify: The combined place values are 8 tens and 3 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: 6 ones + 7 ones = 13 ones. Trade 10 ones for 1 ten, leaving 3 ones. Then 7 tens + 1 regrouped ten = 8 tens, so 56 + 27 = 83. Check: 83 - 27 = 56.

question_audio.text: Add 56 plus 27. What is the sum? A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 56 plus 27, with a total of 83.
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.visual_description: A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 56 plus 27, with a total of 83.
adaptive_question_bank.accessible_description: A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 56 plus 27, with a total of 83.

hints: Focus: In 39 + 25, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add 9 and 5 to get 14 ones; trade 10 ones for 1 ten.
Verify: The combined place values are 6 tens and 4 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: 9 ones + 5 ones = 14 ones. Trade 10 ones for 1 ten, leaving 4 ones. Then 5 tens + 1 regrouped ten = 6 tens, so 39 + 25 = 64. Check: 64 - 25 = 39.

question_audio.text: Add 39 plus 25. What is the sum? An addition model combines 39 and 25 to make 64, regrouping 10 ones as 1 ten.
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.visual_description: An addition model combines 39 and 25 to make 64, regrouping 10 ones as 1 ten.
adaptive_question_bank.accessible_description: An addition model combines 39 and 25 to make 64, regrouping 10 ones as 1 ten.

checkpoint.prompt: The art cart has 15 crayons. A teacher adds 20 more. How many crayons are on the cart now?
hints: Focus: In 15 + 20, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add the ones (5 + 0) and the tens (1 + 2) by place value.
Verify: The combined place values are 3 tens and 5 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

checkpoint.explanation: Combine like place values: 1 tens + 2 tens = 3 tens, and 5 ones + 0 ones = 5 ones. Therefore, 15 + 20 = 35. Check: 35 - 20 = 15.

question_audio.text: The art cart has 15 crayons. A teacher adds 20 more. How many crayons are on the cart now? Base-ten blocks show 15 and 20 as tens and ones to combine into 35.
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.visual_description: Base-ten blocks show 15 and 20 as tens and ones to combine into 35.
checkpoint.accessible_description: Base-ten blocks show 15 and 20 as tens and ones to combine into 35.

checkpoint.prompt: Room 2 has 32 books on one shelf and 7 books on another shelf. How many books are there in all?
hints: Focus: In 32 + 7, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add the ones (2 + 7) and the tens (3 + 0) by place value.
Verify: The combined place values are 3 tens and 9 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

checkpoint.explanation: Combine like place values: 3 tens + 0 tens = 3 tens, and 2 ones + 7 ones = 9 ones. Therefore, 32 + 7 = 39. Check: 39 - 7 = 32.

question_audio.text: Room 2 has 32 books on one shelf and 7 books on another shelf. How many books are there in all? A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 32 plus 7, with a total of 39.
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.visual_description: A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 32 plus 7, with a total of 39.
checkpoint.accessible_description: A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 32 plus 7, with a total of 39.

checkpoint.prompt: Students collected 26 leaves on Monday and 13 leaves on Tuesday. How many leaves did they collect altogether?
hints: Focus: In 26 + 13, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add the ones (6 + 3) and the tens (2 + 1) by place value.
Verify: The combined place values are 3 tens and 9 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

checkpoint.explanation: Combine like place values: 2 tens + 1 tens = 3 tens, and 6 ones + 3 ones = 9 ones. Therefore, 26 + 13 = 39. Check: 39 - 13 = 26.

question_audio.text: Students collected 26 leaves on Monday and 13 leaves on Tuesday. How many leaves did they collect altogether? An addition model combines 26 and 13 to make 39.
question_audio.label: Read Question

checkpoint.visual_description: An addition model combines 26 and 13 to make 39.
checkpoint.accessible_description: An addition model combines 26 and 13 to make 39.

level_banks.label: Level 3: Add Two-Digit Numbers Without Regrouping
level_banks.focus: Add tens and ones without regrouping.

hints: Focus: In 23 + 14, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add the ones (3 + 4) and the tens (2 + 1) by place value.
Verify: The combined place values are 3 tens and 7 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

questions.explanation: Combine like place values: 2 tens + 1 tens = 3 tens, and 3 ones + 4 ones = 7 ones. Therefore, 23 + 14 = 37. Check: 37 - 14 = 23.

question_audio.text: Add 23 plus 14. What is the sum? A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 23 plus 14, with a total of 37.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 23 plus 14, with a total of 37.
questions.accessible_description: A tens-and-ones place-value chart shows 23 plus 14, with a total of 37.

questions.explanation: Combine like place values: 3 tens + 2 tens = 5 tens, and 5 ones + 2 ones = 7 ones. Therefore, 35 + 22 = 57. Check: 57 - 22 = 35.

question_audio.text: Add 35 plus 22. What is the sum? Base-ten blocks show 35 and 22 as tens and ones to combine into 57.

level_banks.label: Level 4: Add Two-Digit Numbers With Regrouping
level_banks.focus: Regroup 10 ones as 1 ten to add.

hints: Focus: In 47 + 36, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add 7 and 6 to get 13 ones; trade 10 ones for 1 ten.
Verify: The combined place values are 8 tens and 3 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

questions.explanation: 7 ones + 6 ones = 13 ones. Trade 10 ones for 1 ten, leaving 3 ones. Then 7 tens + 1 regrouped ten = 8 tens, so 47 + 36 = 83. Check: 83 - 36 = 47.

question_audio.text: Add 47 plus 36. What is the sum? Base-ten blocks show 47 and 36 as tens and ones to combine into 83.
question_audio.label: Read Question

level_banks.label: Mixed
level_banks.focus: Add within 100 using any model or strategy.

questions.prompt: The art cart has 15 crayons. A teacher adds 20 more. How many crayons are on the cart now?
hints: Focus: In 15 + 20, separate each addend into tens and ones.
Strategy: Add the ones (5 + 0) and the tens (1 + 2) by place value.
Verify: The combined place values are 3 tens and 5 ones; check that this matches your sum.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the ones, regroup if they make 10 or more, and then add the tens.

questions.explanation: Combine like place values: 1 tens + 2 tens = 3 tens, and 5 ones + 0 ones = 5 ones. Therefore, 15 + 20 = 35. Check: 35 - 20 = 15.

story.text: Listen to this page. You are starting a math mission about Add Within 100. Look at the story and think about what the characters need to solve. Math missions are easier when you name the numbers and the model first. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Add Within 100. Look at the addition model and connect each part to the math words. Use the vocabulary on the page to explain your thinking. Remember to check place value, units, or symbols before you answer. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This page lets you watch a worked example for Add Within 100. Follow the model one step at a time. Say what each number means before you look at the final answer. If a symbol appears, read it in words like plus, minus, equals, greater than, or less than. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo helps you try Add Within 100 with support. Build or study the model first, then connect it to the question. A good strategy is to explain the answer in math words before choosing. Press Next when you are ready for practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the visual model to help you think about Add Within 100. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
practice.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your mission work on Add Within 100. Look at your stars and remember the strategy you used. Mistakes help show what to practice next. Save your growth data to continue.

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth for Add Within 100. Look at attempts, correct answers, accuracy, and hints used. Use the recommendation to decide what to practice next. You can continue to Skill Practice or replay the mission.
